purpose: Test whether KeyAI can represent an unfamiliar public Lean repository through a thin declarative boundary while holding the verifier fixed and changing the domain and repository structure.
research_question: Is the current KeyAI substrate portable enough to support meaningful external discovery, or is it still an ECDLP-specific repository system?

evidence_boundary.statement: This rehearsal may establish technical portability or expose technical blockers. It cannot establish user pain, workflow value, retention, willingness to pay, or external pilot completion.

allowed: Score public Lean repositories against the fixed selection rubric.
Clone one selected and one reserve public repository at immutable commits.
Run the selected repository's documented dependency, cache, build, and test commands in a disposable local checkout under an explicit reviewed-public-source trust boundary.
Read and hash tracked files, parse source-level imports and declaration headings, and record explicit trust signals.
Write KeyAI-side manifests, snapshots, compatibility evidence, tests, and architecture updates.
Refactor only the smallest KeyAI boundary justified by observed portability failures.
prohibited: Modify, commit to, fork for publication, open an issue in, or contact maintainers of the inspected repository.
Treat repository metadata or a successful build as customer or external pilot evidence.
Insert this rehearsal into repo/PILOT_PROTOCOL.json evidence_log.
Unlock TASK-012 or change CH-001 or CH-002 status from rehearsal evidence.
Run a new cryptanalytic, secp256k1, or other research hypothesis.
Build hosted product infrastructure, authentication, billing, collaboration, or a second verifier adapter.
Hide unsupported files, parser limitations, build failures, or project-specific code behind a successful summary.
authority.source_checkout_policy: The canonical inspection checkout must be clean, remain byte-for-byte unchanged, and match the full selected commit SHA before and after every run. Declared build effects are confined to a separate disposable checkout.
authority.license_policy: Record the repository license and license file. Commit only KeyAI-authored metadata, hashes, summaries, and source-level identifiers needed for reproducibility; do not vendor the inspected repository.
authority.secrets_policy: Use only reviewed public source data. The compiled probe passes a fixed environment allowlist with no credential-like variables and uses disposable HOME/TEMP paths. This host does not provide OS-enforced filesystem isolation, and project-controlled lake env output is not independently authenticated, so arbitrary untrusted repository execution remains prohibited and both conditions fail the BUILD gate.

required: Recognized open-source license with a repository license file
Substantive multi-module source rather than a tutorial or toy
No sensitive or private data requirement

scored_dimensions.definition: Meaningfully different mathematical or software domain while Lean remains fixed.
Exercises imports, namespaces, generated/config files, CI, and nontrivial module layout.
Pinned toolchain and documented commands can be reproduced at the selected commit.
License, ownership, source roots, and project intent are explicit.
The project was not designed around KeyAI and is not maintained by this repository owner.
The checkout and build are large enough to be meaningful but bounded for one rehearsal.

candidates.build_command: cd cedar-lean && lake -R -Kenv=dev update && lake build Cedar SymCC && lake lint
candidates.activity_signal: Selected commit authored 2026-07-23; repository CI documents Lean build, lint, tests, FFI, protobuf, and documentation jobs.
candidates.approximate_scale: 828 tracked files; 351 tracked Lean files in a nested Lean project inside a Rust/Lean/protobuf/FFI repository.
candidates.selection_notes: Best controlled stress test: executable policy semantics and proofs, nested project root, multiple Lean libraries, CI lint, symbolic links, and non-Lean infrastructure without requiring the DRT/FFI build.

candidates.build_command: lake exe cache get && lake build
candidates.activity_signal: Selected commit authored 2026-07-23; active community repository with build and linter workflows.
candidates.approximate_scale: 754 tracked files; 676 tracked Lean files across Physlib, PhyslibAlpha, and QuantumInfo.
candidates.selection_notes: Strong reserve for a large Mathlib-based scientific library; lower first-rehearsal value because its conventional single-root layout stresses less repository integration while its cached dependency surface is larger.

candidates.build_command: lake exe cache get && lake build
candidates.activity_signal: Pinned default-branch commit authored 2026-07-23; contributing guide documents the complete cached build.
candidates.selection_notes: Excellent corpus-scale test, but the large statement-oriented Mathlib surface is less diagnostic of a thin repository adapter than Cedar's mixed executable/proof layout.

candidates.activity_signal: Pinned default-branch commit authored 2026-07-06; README documents independent library, executable, theory, and verification targets.
candidates.selection_notes: A compact and deep metatheory test, but it changes both repository conventions and trust-level subject matter, making first-failure attribution less clean.

candidates.build_command: lake exe cache get && lake build
candidates.activity_signal: Pinned default-branch commit authored 2026-07-20; root README documents the cached build.
candidates.approximate_scale: 12,685 tracked files; 9,449 tracked Lean files across several nested projects and toolchains.
candidates.selection_notes: Maximum structural stress, but multiple nested toolchains and a very large tree would make the first rehearsal expensive and blur thin-adapter failures with monorepo policy.

candidates.build_command: lake exe cache get! && lake build
candidates.activity_signal: Pinned default-branch commit authored 2026-07-23; README documents complete local verification and expected axiom output.
candidates.approximate_scale: 172 tracked files; 120 tracked Lean files plus a linked mathematical blueprint.
candidates.selection_notes: Strong proof-corpus candidate with explicit trust guidance, but its conventional Mathlib project layout exercises fewer repository-boundary assumptions.

candidates.build_command: lake exe cache get && lake build
candidates.activity_signal: Pinned default-branch commit authored 2026-02-18; README documents OpenBLAS prerequisites and the build.
candidates.approximate_scale: 605 tracked files; 568 tracked Lean files with native numerical dependencies.
candidates.selection_notes: Valuable future native-dependency test, but the repository explicitly says Windows is not officially supported, so this host would confound adapter and environment failures.

candidates.build_command: lake exe cache get && lake build
candidates.activity_signal: Pinned default-branch commit authored 2026-07-16; README documents the cached Lean build.
candidates.approximate_scale: 121 tracked files; 72 tracked Lean files plus a blueprint.
candidates.selection_notes: Highly bounded and reproducible, but too close to a conventional single-library Mathlib project to expose the architecture assumptions this rehearsal is intended to test.

selected_source.build_command: cd cedar-lean && lake -R -Kenv=dev update && lake build Cedar SymCC && lake lint

documented_workspace_effect.reason: The env=dev branch adds documentation dependencies in the disposable build workspace. No canonical source or KeyAI-owned external source copy was changed.

accepted_axiom_groups.rationale: Lean 4.31 materializes these exact native_decide results at the pinned commit as private generated axioms whose trust rests on the native compiler. Exact names and Lean-environment provenance are required; a repository-wide wildcard is not accepted.

baseline.environment: Windows x86_64, 5828.95 MiB physical memory, elan 4.2.3, Lean 4.31.0, Lake 5.0.0; LEAN_NUM_THREADS=1; canonical LF checkout semantics for upstream lint.
commands: cd cedar-lean && LEAN_NUM_THREADS=1 lake build Cedar SymCC
baseline.result: Passed with explicit host constraints: the 571-job Cedar/SymCC build and canonical-LF lint succeeded; Lean's actual 273-module closure matched the lexical closure, all 3,423 source-public headings resolved inside 12,296 public compiled constants, and the exact axiom policy passed.

adapter_contract.input: A clean checkout at the selected full commit SHA plus one declarative source adapter embedded in selected_source.
adapter_contract.output: A deterministic KeyAI-authored source snapshot plus a separate compiled-environment probe for the selected build closure. The source snapshot inventories every tracked file; the compiled probe resolves public declarations and reports transitive axiom dependencies.
invariants: No KeyAI command writes the canonical inspection checkout; build and compiled-probe work occurs only in a disposable workspace.
Every tracked source path receives exactly one explicit classification.
Every in-scope Lean file maps to exactly one module.
Files outside configured module roots remain visible as lean_out_of_scope.
Unknown project semantics remain unknown and are never converted into claims, tasks, decisions, or verified results.
Build evidence remains separate from source-level observations.
Compiled declaration and axiom claims come from the pinned Lean environment, never from the lexical source parser alone.
Project-launched compiled evidence cannot support BUILD or arbitrary untrusted intake until the launcher, module search path, loaded artifacts, and output channel are independently controlled.
The snapshot is deterministic and contains no current timestamps, absolute paths, usernames, credentials, or machine-specific checkout paths.
The generic implementation contains no branch on repository owner, repository name, project name, or mathematical domain.
forbidden_architecture: Per-project conditional branches in the generic inspector
Synthetic verified ledger or task queue
Network dependency in the ordinary KeyAI CI gate
Automatic public-site publication of rehearsal results

metrics.target: 100% of git-tracked files receive one classification
100% of configured in-scope Lean files map to one module; out-of-scope Lean files are listed
Two clean runs at the same commit produce identical snapshot SHA-256 values
Zero changes in the canonical inspection checkout before and after every run; only declared upstream update effects may appear in disposable build workspaces
Zero repository-, owner-, project-, or domain-name conditionals in the generic inspector
100% tracked-file classification, zero generator-reported unsupported Lean files, and explicit parser limitations; this metric does not claim lexical completeness for every Lean construct
The selected pinned source completes its documented baseline build, or the exact external blocker is recorded
All existing KeyAI Python, generated-fixpoint, repository, and Lean CI gates remain green

For this reviewed public source, the project-launched Lean probe enumerates every public constant in the Cedar/SymCC build closure; every source-inventoried public heading resolves; no sorryAx or unexpected custom axiom is present; exact compiler-trust dependencies and provenance are enumerated. Independent launcher/output authenticity is a separate required gate.
Before arbitrary untrusted intake, external Lean execution has OS-enforced filesystem and network isolation in addition to an environment allowlist and disposable workspace
Before arbitrary untrusted intake, the trusted Lean launcher, module search path, loaded artifacts, and probe output channel are controlled independently of the inspected project
Zero edits to the generic inspector or generator between the first and second snapshot run

build: The result is useful as a technical demonstration without implying customer validation.
change: The generic core remains plausible, but one or more bounded adapter, provenance, or execution-boundary gaps prevent a build disposition.
Every gap has an exact owner, smallest next test, stop condition, and no public product claim.
stop: The source requires project-specific generator logic or modification of the inspected repository.
The snapshot would materially misrepresent the source or verifier state.
The required architecture exceeds a thin Lean adapter and would amount to speculative platform construction.
decision_rules.precedence: Stop conditions override build. Build requires every required metric to pass; otherwise choose change or stop.
decision_rules.task_012_rule: No rehearsal disposition unlocks TASK-012. Only the latest valid completed external CH-001 discovery disposition in repo/PILOT_PROTOCOL.json may do so.

gaps.smallest_next_test: Run one bounded hostile fixture in the proposed runner that attempts host-secret reads, source and path-escape writes, process spawning, network access, and timeout or output-limit exhaustion.
gaps.stop_condition: Stop portability expansion if any hostile action escapes containment or if containment requires project-specific execution logic.

gaps.smallest_next_test: Run one hostile fixture through a trusted absolute Lean launcher with a controlled module search path and loaded-artifact binding; the fixture must attempt both forged KEYAI output and probe-module shadowing.
gaps.stop_condition: Stop portability expansion if the trusted runner cannot reject forged output and shadowed probe modules without project-specific logic or modification of the inspected repository.

change_plan.scope_guard: Do not unlock TASK-012, publish a product claim, or start an ECDLP hypothesis from this technical rehearsal.

decision.summary: The thin declarative Lean boundary passed source, baseline, compiled, determinism, adversarial, and KeyAI non-regression checks, but BUILD remains blocked by the two required execution-boundary failures: no OS-enforced isolation and no launcher/output authenticity independent of the inspected project.
